message:
fix(tools): empty-attachment guard, reservation index doc/test updates, clippy lint suppression

In messaging.rs:
- Guard against empty attachment_paths vec (previously only checked
  for None, so Some(&[]) would enter the attachment validation loop
  and potentially canonicalize a nonexistent base directory).
- Add #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] to validate_message_size_limits.
- Refactor match on canonical_base to idiomatic map_or_else.

In reservation_index.rs:
- Update module-level and method doc comments to reflect the current
  lookup strategy: exact paths use ancestor/descendant lookups while
  globs remain prefix-scoped with root-glob fallback.
- Add test glob_request_conflicts_with_exact_directory_anchor verifying
  that a glob like src/**/*.rs correctly conflicts with an exact
  reservation on the bare directory anchor "src".

In reservations.rs:
- Update doc comment to match the new index lookup description.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -477,10 +477,9 @@ pub async fn file_reservation_paths(
 
     let mut pending_conflicts: Vec<PendingReservationConflict> = Vec::new();
 
-    // Build prefix-partitioned index from exclusive reservations held by other
-    // agents. This replaces the O(M×N) brute-force loop with prefix-scoped
-    // lookups: only reservations sharing a first path segment (or root globs)
-    // are examined per request.
+    // Build the reservation index from exclusive reservations held by other
+    // agents. Exact paths now use exact/ancestor/descendant lookups, while
+    // glob reservations remain prefix-scoped with a small root-glob fallback.
     let index = ReservationIndex::build(
         active
             .iter()
